_doc: GREENLOOP state — Case Study 001 run. Companion to GREENLOOP.md v2.4.0.
user_request: We need to create Greenloop Case Study 001. Goal: choose a small real coding task, run it through Greenloop end to end, and produce a public proof artifact. Do not expand the concept. Reduce it into evidence.
goal: Enforce the DEPLOY.md mirror invariant ('mirror any workflow change into the embedded payloads in cli/greenloop-inject.ts') with byte-comparison parity tests, so drift between workflow/ and the embedded constants fails the suite instead of relying on discipline.
scope_fence: cli/greenloop-inject.test.ts (add tests only), .greenloop/ (state), docs/CASE-STUDY-001.md, README.md + index.html (link the case study only). NOT in scope: editing cli/greenloop-inject.ts, workflow/*, install.sh, SHA256SUMS, deleting legacy src/, any deploy.
constraints: Add tests; do not modify the injector source or workflow payloads.
Match existing test-file style (node:test, double quotes, no semicolons).
README/site changes limited to linking the case study.

dod.check: 4 new parity tests exist, each byte-comparing one embedded payload const to its workflow/ file (modulo leading/trailing whitespace)
dod.evidence: suite section 11 'embedded payload parity with workflow/': 4/4 pass at 2026-07-04T00:51Z

dod.check: Falsifiability proven: a 1-byte mutation of workflow/GREENLOOP.md makes the parity test FAIL; restoring makes it PASS
dod.evidence: printf 'X' >> workflow/GREENLOOP.md → 88 pass 1 fail, 'GREENLOOP_CORE has drifted... First difference at line 808: on disk "X"'; restore → 89 pass 0 fail, git diff clean on workflow/

dod.check: Full suite green: node --experimental-strip-types --test cli/greenloop-inject.test.ts → 89 tests, 0 fail (baseline was 85/85)
docs/CASE-STUDY-001.md written (task, state, decisions, verification, outcome) and linked from README.md and index.html
dod.evidence: docs/CASE-STUDY-001.md + docs/case-study-001/{state.json,plan.md,worklog.md} snapshot; README links in intro + layout tree; index.html link in 'Get the files' section

assumptions.assumption: The test suite already enforces embedded-payload ↔ workflow/ parity somewhere
evidence: grep 'workflow/' cli/greenloop-inject.test.ts → only version-string regex checks (sections 9–10)
assumptions.falsifier: grep the test file for content comparison against workflow/ files

assumptions.assumption: The embedded payloads have drifted from workflow/ (naive extractor showed all 4 differing, embedded 72099 vs disk 45503 chars for GREENLOOP_CORE)
evidence: First diff-check run: DRIFT reported on all 4 constants
Suspicious pattern: every 'first diff' line ended in a stray backtick → checker bug hypothesis
assumptions.falsifier: Fix the extractor to stop at an unescaped trailing backtick and unescape \` \${ \\, then re-compare

assumptions.assumption: The existing extractConst helper in the test file can extract the workflow payload constants
evidence: extractConst terminates only on a lone-backtick line; GREENLOOP_CORE et al. close with a trailing backtick on the last content line → it over-collects into subsequent constants
assumptions.falsifier: Read extractConst; check termination condition against how the 4 payload constants close

assumptions.assumption: Unescaping \` then \${ then \\ reproduces the exact on-disk bytes of all 4 workflow files
evidence: Corrected checker: OK on all 4 pairs — GREENLOOP_CORE 45503, APPENDICES 6794, SCHEMA 1871, PROFILE_DESIGN 13037 chars, trim-equal
assumptions.falsifier: Byte-compare after unescape; any mismatch falsifies

steps.desc: Append section 11 to cli/greenloop-inject.test.ts: extractPayload helper (unescaped-trailing-backtick termination + unescape) and 4 parity tests
Mutation check: append 1 byte to workflow/GREENLOOP.md, run suite, expect the GREENLOOP_CORE parity test to FAIL; restore file; expect green
steps.verify: suite fails during mutation, passes after restore, git diff clean on workflow/

steps.desc: Write docs/CASE-STUDY-001.md; add one link in README.md and one in index.html
steps.verify: file exists; README and index.html each reference CASE-STUDY-001.md

failures.error: Naive extractor reported DRIFT on all 4 embedded payloads (phantom ~26KB drift)
failures.rca: Extractor assumed the closing backtick sits on its own line (copied from test-file extractConst); the payload constants close with a trailing backtick on the last content line, so extraction ran past the const boundary into unrelated source
failures.resolution: Terminate extraction at an unescaped trailing backtick; unescape template-literal escapes. Re-run → all 4 pairs identical. Drift assumption falsified before any edit was made.

decisions.what: Chosen proof task: add embedded-payload parity tests
decisions.why: DEPLOY.md states the mirror invariant but nothing enforces it — the suite only checks version strings. A parity test converts prose doctrine into a falsifiable check. Small, finite, real.
decisions.alternatives_rejected: Delete legacy untracked src/ + root copies (housekeeping, no verification story); fix phantom payload drift (falsified — there is no drift); touch install.sh/SHA256SUMS (verified clean, sha256sum -c OK)

decisions.what: New extractPayload helper in the test file instead of modifying existing extractConst
decisions.why: extractConst is load-bearing for 85 passing tests and its lone-backtick termination is correct for the hook/plugin constants it serves; changing it risks the existing suite for zero gain
decisions.alternatives_rejected: Patch extractConst to handle both terminations (wider blast radius); export the constants from greenloop-inject.ts (source edit — out of scope fence)

convergence.target: Unenforced mirror invariant: embedded payload constants in cli/greenloop-inject.ts vs workflow/ files
convergence.done_when: node --experimental-strip-types --test cli/greenloop-inject.test.ts exits 0 with 89 tests passing (4 new parity tests), AND a deliberate 1-byte mutation of workflow/GREENLOOP.md makes the suite fail, AND restoring the file returns it to green with workflow/ unchanged per git diff.

verification.last_independent_check: mutation check 2026-07-04T00:51Z: 1-byte perturbation → 88 pass 1 fail; restore → 89 pass, workflow/ diff clean
